red_baseline.reason: T013 froze the shared low-risk lifecycle, reuse/concurrency, ownership, partial restart, indeterminate run, and forbidden-input contract.
red_baseline.summary: Three no-effect refusal tests pass; five lifecycle tests fail because createTaskHandlers has no mutation handlers.

validation.summary: 8/8 shared lifecycle, one-spawn convergence, refusal, partial restart, and indeterminate run contracts passed.

validation.command: focused Kernel, journal, lifecycle, ownership, CLI, and repaired inspect regressions
validation.summary: Kernel/journal suites and all focused lifecycle compatibility cases passed after repairing legacy refusal mirrors, transaction identity, restart authority, and port detail projection.

validation.command: two concurrent real CLI starts against one isolated managed fixture
validation.summary: Both calls returned the same run ID; the first reported changed=true, the second reusedExistingRun=true and changed=false, with exactly one spawn receipt.
Syntax check passed for 80 JavaScript files.
230 total; 229 passed; 1 existing POSIX platform skip; 0 failures; 196.4 seconds.
All T014 source changes passed whitespace validation.

review.evidence: Low-risk start/dev/run/aliases/explicit stop/restart now enter one Application Kernel path with dynamic policy revalidation and canonical locks; existing lifecycle authorities remain the sole effect providers; force, risky, stop-all, and follow controls remain CLI-only; real concurrent starts converge on one spawn receipt; final CLI schemaVersion 1 and full-suite regressions are green.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Kernel handlers delegate to the existing config, runtime state, global run index, ownership proof, events, locks, and process lifecycle authorities; no parallel backend was added.
Kernel resolves current task risk and permits public mutation only for low-risk tasks; medium, high, destructive, unknown, and external cases refuse before effects.
Migrated normal JSON retains legacy mirrors and adds only data.agentResult; compact, text, stable codes, details, next actions, and exit meanings remain compatible.
Operation journal preparation precedes lifecycle effects, matching starts converge by durable state rather than replay, and indeterminate foreground execution remains reconcile-only.
All public low-risk lifecycle mutations execute as Application Kernel handlers after project, compatibility, risk, ownership, journal, and lock checks.
Sequential, synchronized Kernel, and real concurrent CLI starts produce one run and one spawn receipt with honest reuse and changed semantics.
Unknown and external ownership stays inspect-only; legacy CLI refusal codes/actions are projections of the same no-effect Kernel result.
Task risk comes from the trusted config-backed resolver and is re-evaluated under the ordered mutation locks immediately before effects.
Public mutation schema rejects command, cwd, environment, force, confirmation, path, remote, and policy override fields recursively before effects.
The same low-only Kernel risk gate protects start, stop, restart, and run; permissive CLI annotations cannot broaden it.
Adapter trusted context supplies scope only and cannot inject approval, force, ownership, or risk authority.
Managed processes remain independent of adapter lifetime and keep the existing run/state identity and observation path.
Uncertain finite completion is persisted as indeterminate and returns operation.reconcile without invoking the run provider again.
Lifecycle routing never invokes adoption inspection, registration, config writes, or any implicit discovery-to-start chain.
Kernel, CLI, and existing lifecycle regressions normalize to the same semantic outcomes while preserving explicitly allowed CLI formatting and provenance differences.

recovery.action: Repaired an accidental restart closure placement, legacy refusal code/next/details mirrors, transaction identity, and global restart authority; after each repair reran the originally selected focused validations, then reran the complete serialized suite.
